entry.title: Splash Screen - SAP BTP Mobile Service - Not Working - MDK 24.11.3
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am currently trying to implement the use of a Splash Screen in an application built on SAP BTP Mobile Service, but despite following all the reference documentation, I have not been able to get it to work correctly.

https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/getting-started/mdk/custom-client/app-icon-splash-screen.html

I used the following configuration to set up the launcher and splash screen icons and applied it in the MDKProject.json file as instructed.

I’m using the following configuration to load the launcher and splash screen icons. I'm also adding the image folder using the same names and path as specified in the documentation.

However, neither the launcher icon nor the splash screen background appear in the app, even though everything seems to be set up as described. The project was built successfully without errors.

@robinkuck @bill_froelich @Jitendra_Kansal78 

Thank you very much for your help. If any additional information is needed, I remain at your disposal.

NEW UPDATE:

SDK versions currently available in Mobile Services

entry.title: Re: Publish views from POD's to other users
entry.content:
@ManoelCosta 

Indeed I can set the default per POD, but i have one POD per process but have multiple workcenters and per workcenter multiple resources.
So I want to prevent to create one POD per workcenter-resource combination and this seemed to work with the views.

entry.title: ATC - Sap readiness check for 2023 S4 - error The SAP-owned namespace /0SJ1P/ is in scope. SAP objec
entry.content:
Hello All - 

 

we are running ATC for custom objects in brownfield approach , we are getting error as below - 
The SAP-owned namespace /0SJ1P/ is in scope. SAP objects cannot be checked in this case.

 

these error are appearing , when we are running for VOFM routein .. 
now the challenge is  - /0SJ1P/ in table "TRNSPACE " is have following values - 

NAMESPACE /0SJ1P/

ROLE P
LICENSE
EDITFLAG X
SSCRFLAG
SAPFLAG X
GEN ONLY
MT TYPE

i ve checked multiple blogs , there has been no solution provide people has tried multiple things , but still a final solution and approach is missing .


thanks

entry.title: Re: Discount % in Goods Issue
entry.content:
Hi @Underdog ,

I assume you are entering prices and totals for the items in the Goods Issue. If you copy and paste or input the price and also enter the total manually, SAP will adjust the fields to match the total, using a discount if necessary. Normally, you input quantity and unit price, and SAP computes the total based on quantity multiplied by price. Below are two scenarios: in the first, the total has been entered manually, while in the second, it has not.



Check if that was your case. I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Publish views from POD's to other users
entry.content:
I see! Perhaps you can evaluate creating desktop shortcuts or browser bookmarks for your PODs, including WCs/Resources in the URL like the example below:

https://xxxxxxx.execution.eu20-prod.web.dmc.cloud.sap/cp.portal/site#DMEWorkCenterPOD-Display
?sap-ui-app-id-hint=sap.dm.dme.pod&/?=&WORKCENTER=DCBR-A1&RESOURCE=BR-ASSY001

Hope it helps! Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: MDG Custom entity refresh on change, Empty custom entity
entry.content: Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: Custom Code decommission - Transaction usage
entry.content: Hi David - we did check ST03N , bt there was no usage reported over there so it seems program was never called till selection screen tooo.. but we are checking with Business to get confirmation if transaction is called as Batch Job but not as dialog user .. can you provide some insight if the program is called via different program will SCMON and SAP Decommission Cockpit will be able to capture is as used or not being used ?

entry.title: Re: SAP Information Steward Task Failing - "Invalid colum...
entry.content:
Hi Joe,

Thanks for the response.

I checked the database and found the following:

  1. The column IS_GEN_SOURCE_ROWID is present in the <TABLE_ALIAS>_SOURCE table.
  2. However, this column is missing in <TABLE_ALIAS>_FR, and I couldn't locate the <TABLE_ALIAS>_SR table in the database.
  3. Also, the " Delete Failed Data and Run " option is greyed out/ not available.

entry.title: Return of Inventory in Transit
entry.content:
Hello Expert, 

I have the following scenario:

I generated a transfer order with delivery confirmation for 10 units.

The Good issue was made with the 10 units from the source document and was Posted.

When the merchandise arrived at the warehouse, (PO Good Receipt) only 8 units were received.

The question is, how can I return the 2 units that are in transit?

entry.title: Cannot update Picklist after updating source document
entry.content:
Hi, 

i ve got a problem and i need help. I am increasing picked quantity (over the released quantity) on picklist via service layer so I need to increase quantity on Sales order.

For Info:
Article has no prescribed batches on document before PATCH request and is managed using batch numbers and warehouse has NO BinLocations (Disabled BinLocations) 

I do PATCH /b1s/v2/Orders(1111)

{
  "DocEntry": 1413,
  "DocNum": 1111,
  "DocObjectCode": "oOrders",
  "DocumentsOwner": 5,
  "SalesPersonCode": 1,
  "DocDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
  "DocDueDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
  "TaxDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
  "CardCode": "Test",
  "CardName": "Test Test",
  "NumAtCard": "",
  "PaymentGroupCode": 4,
  "ReserveInvoice": "tNO",
  "AddressExtension": {
    "ShipToStreet": "Test",
    "ShipToBuilding": "",
    "ShipToCity": "Praha",
    "ShipToZipCode": "111 11",
    "ShipToCountry": "CZ",
    "BillToStreet": "Test",
    "BillToBuilding": "",
    "BillToCity": "Praha",
    "BillToZipCode": "111 11",
    "BillToCountry": "CZ",
    "DocEntry": 1413
  },
  "DocumentLines": [
    {
      "LineNum": 0,
      "VisualOrder": 0,
      "LineStatus": "bost_Open",
      "BaseType": -1,
      "ItemCode": "B3002",
      "ItemDescription": "Desc",
      "ShipDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
      "WarehouseCode": "01",
      "UseBaseUnits": "tNO",
      "RemainingOpenQuantity": 5.0,
      "InventoryQuantity": 5.2,
      "RemainingOpenInventoryQuantity": 5.0,
      "U_VAG_WHP_OriginalQtty": 4.0,
      "MeasureUnit": "",
      "UnitsOfMeasurment": 1.0,
      "UoMEntry": -1,
      "UoMCode": "Manuálně",
      "BatchNumbers": [
        {
          "BatchNumber": "AAA",
          "BaseLineNumber": 0,
          "Quantity": 5.2,
          "SystemSerialNumber": 2,
          "ItemCode": "TestItem"
        }
      ],
      "SerialNumbers": [],
      "DocumentLinesBinAllocations": [],
      "DocumentLineAdditionalExpenses": []
    }
  ],
  "DocumentAdditionalExpenses": [],
  "DocumentSpecialLines": []
}

PATCH request will Succeed and after that i try to update picklist 
PUT  /b1s/v2/PickLists(679)
{
    "Absoluteentry": 679,
    "Name": "Manager",
    "ObjectType": "156",
    "OwnerCode": 1,
    "OwnerName": null,
    "PickDate": "2025-07-01T00:00:00Z",
    "Status": "ps_Released",
    "UseBaseUnits": "tNO",
    "PickListsLines": [
        {
            "AbsoluteEntry": 679,
            "LineNumber": 0,
            "OrderEntry": 1413,
            "OrderRowID": 0,
            "PickedQuantity": 5.2,
            "ReleasedQuantity": 0,
            "BaseObjectType": 17,
            "SerialNumbers": [],
            "BatchNumbers": [
                {
                    "BatchNumber": "AAA",
                    "BaseLineNumber": 0,
                    "SystemSerialNumber": 2,
                    "Quantity": 5.2,
                    "ItemCode": "TestItem"
                }
            ],
            "DocumentLinesBinAllocations": []
        }
    ]
}

Result =>
{
    "error": {
        "code": "-2039",
        "details": [
            {
                "code": "",
                "message": ""
            }
        ],
        "message": "Another user or another operation modified data; to continue, open the window again (ODBC -2039)"
    }
}

When i do the same with warehouse with bin locations and with aditional /b1s/v2/PickListsService_UpdateReleasedAllocation call, request will be OK, 
if i do the same with warehouse without bin locations and without batches (only PATCH Orders, PUT Picklists) request will be ok. 
What i should do different. I tried it also with PickListsService_UpdateReleasedAllocation call (the same ERROR), i tried PATCH instead of PUT (the same ERROR).
If you ve got any advice, any solution, i will be glad. 
Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Prorate based on Months not days
entry.content: I tested this and it works perfect for list prices already in the Lookup table. We have the requirement that we need this also to work the same way for manual override prices in the subscription. If a change occure for a Yearly billing cycle jJanuary 1 to December 31st, and from April 1st the qty is changed. Then this proration does not work, only when using list price from the lookup table, not for the overridable price. Any suggestion for this?

entry.title: Transfer historical unbilled revenue to Enteprise project
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have a cost based POC project in EPPM and i'm trying to move historical unbilled revenue from older system to New public cloud.

Can you tell how can i create a GL account entry to add unbilled revenue against my project so that it shows in event based revenue recognition. Sharing the template in attachment

Thanks,

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: SAP Customer Checkout Credit Card Refunds not shown in SAP B1 Deposits window
entry.content: Hi candrade_0518, SAP B1 provide the option to register an Incoming Payment with negative amounts based on a A/R Credit Memo or a negative A/R Invoice. So, by saying this it is possible in SAP B1 to do a credit card refund and been able to select it in the Deposit to bank window. The only thing here that I'm realizing is that SCCO integration is not doing that even if SAP B1 permits it by standard. Think about it, doing a gross deposit (without refunds) and afterwards in the bank reconciliation start looking for all credit card refunds that should be part of different deposits will be an uphill and difficult approach

entry.title: Re: connecting to success factors from SOAP API fails
entry.content: Hi Cyril, yes, it was the date range and data available, got it fixed thank you.

entry.title: Re: Budget scenarion
entry.content:
Hello,

I do see you are using the Correct Fiori App. "Manage Budget Scenarios" (App ID: F2129)

Check other settings and do some due diligence as below:-

A. Check Roles and Authorizations - If its an authorization issue.

You need roles like:

SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT

SAP_BR_BPC_CONTROLLER

SAP_BR_OVERHEAD_ACCNTNT

Use the "Maintain Business Roles" app to check if the user has these roles.

B. Check for Missing Configuration Steps

Besides initializing, verify:

1. Ledger and Controlling Area settings are correctly linked.

2. Fiscal Year Variant is properly set.

3. Budget Profile and Version are defined.

4. Controlling Area is not correctly assigned to company codes.

5. Planning versions (like 0 or 1) are not active.

I hope this helps you to resolve the issues.

 

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: SQ02 join LIPS and EKPO
entry.content:
Hello

I need to provide a query to business who needs to check inbound deliveries at item level with some specific data that are only at PO item level

LIPS.VGPOS and EKPO.EBELP cannot be jointed because I guess there is a format issue

Does anybody know how to correctly add code inside SQ02 in order to force this joint (I tried in DB02 and the SQL code works fine)

I tried some silly code like this but it does not work

Do you know which section I should use and how to write a better code please ?

Should I also add the table here ?

Thank you very much

entry.title: Re: how to throw message in webdynpro
entry.content:
Hi Simon,

This works perfectly! I double checked the allowed method for View parameter and now understood what you meant. Thank you so much for your help!

entry.title: fixed text ME53N
entry.content: Text of the destination deposit field in the RC, has the current information in S4 - as Supplier Center. We need it to be corrected to the correct information, which is Stock Output Warehouse. According to the attached file, we saw that this is a fixed text in the program, making it necessary to correct the translation of the text into Brazilian Portuguese

recorrected my  name   certification name is not the  right name assigned the paper 

the certification name say AYELIGN MEKONNEN  ---- MEKONNEN he is my  grand father not my father. so the middle name of my father TIGABU is missed from the paper. 

The right name is AYELIGN TIGABU MEKONNEN 

regards

entry.title: Re: Splash Screen - SAP BTP Mobile Service - Not Working...
entry.content: That feature was introduced in MDK 25.6.0.   Please upgrade to this version and try again.

entry.title: Re: SFTP Authentication Setup in SAP BTP Integration Suite: A Step-by-Step Guide.
entry.content: very helpful

entry.title: Re: Build and Run Multi-Tenant SaaS Solutions on the SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi Aleksei,

you need a partner or customer BTP global account with at least two subaccounts to deploy the partner reference application:

  • 1 provider subaccount to deploy the multi-tenant app
  • 1 consumer subaccount to subscribe the multi-tenant app

Configuring the connection with some ERP system as well as further consumer subaccounts are optional. 

Most of the quotas for BTP service such as HANA Could and Cloud Foundry are required in the provider subaccount. You find a full list in our GitHub repository on page Bill of Materials.

Best regards,
Knut

entry.title: Re: Event when Workitem is generated for a User
entry.content:
@Sandra_Rossi 

The Methods you have Posted were really Helpful. During the Debugging I found an Event already being raised in the Method CREATE_VIA_WFM, Which I could also use. 

I tried to Map this event to a Custom Handler in SWE2, it did not work because this class does not have any IF_WORKFLOW in it.  

Do you see any possibility to use this Event ? or Create a Listener of this Event.

Best Regards

Ketan

entry.title: Re: A Step-By-Step Walkthrough for SAP Analytics Cloud compass (PART II)
entry.content:
hi @Jaishankar_SP !

In compass, the driver definition is derived from the model definition (e.g. formulas and hierarchies used).
Compass does not create new formulas between measures which have no relationship in the model. Such a base measure will therefore have no other drivers besides itself per model definition. Hence, it is not possible to simulate using different drivers (e.g. the impact of uncertainties in price and volume on revenue), but you could still simulate within the same measure (e.g. the impact on final global FTE, if FTE is fluctuating in different countries.)
I am not sure which menu you are referring to with Insert → Simulation → Compass. If you could provide screenshots it would be great.

Cheers!
Chen Nee

entry.title: Re: Open and close posting periods for at ledger level
entry.content: Thanks for solution

entry.title: Re: Backup file in SAP SucessFactors
entry.content:
Hi @karthik1gobburu ,

Probably your customer will not be able to check the snapshot of the instance backup data.

The available alternative, in case you need to restore any missing configuration and therefore all instance snapshot at that specific moment, is to request for a instance Restore, considering a 30-day backwards window from the day you'll be creating the request, based on the automated backup performed and stored by SF team.

According to below KBA, SF team keeps up to 30 days of backup, and based on the customer needs, any point in time of this period can be used as reference to request the restore.

2218169 - Data Backup Procedures and Restore Process - SuccessFactors Cloud

The KBA you have mentioned (2568976) can be used to backup all instance information for a larger period, upon customer request for this specific activity.

So, currently the options related to SF Backup are:

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/0002218169/E - Automated backup Process, always considering 30 days prior to the current day, customer does not need to request it to be available for Restore request. Text extracted from the KBA: "The goal is to have the ability to restore data at any point in time within the 30 day window."
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/2568976 - Planned Backup that must be planned by the customer and requested with a ticket, allowing a larger backup period (30/60/90 days). 

Best Regards.

Thiago Eva

entry.title: Re: Unable to call Update API using http request
entry.content: I think I know the answer. Put your code inside the Code Block for better readability and I'll help you

entry.title: Re: SAP Sybase ASE Audit Logs: Handling Multi-Row Single Events in Query Results
entry.content: You may get a targeted set of experts by using an ASE tag "SAP Adaptive Server Enterprise". The tags you have selected are not ASE related.

entry.title: The future Extended Producer Responsibility schema for Textile products in Spain
entry.content:
Introduction

Clothing, footwear, household textiles and other textile products, once worn for a certain period of time become waste. The fashion industrym, the current patterns of outfit renovation and the current quality and prices of textile products make these wastes increasingly present. In many cases, when users dispose of them, these products are either in good condition, so they are potentially reusable, or otherwise, they can be passed to recycling circuits.

As prevention is the first priority for waste, once textile waste is generated, it is appropriate to collect it separately so that those in an appropriate state can be prepared for reuse.  Textiles, footwear and complements must be separated at source and subsequently delivered in the proper containers.

The consumption channels for textile products can be defies as:

  • Household textile waste. These waste comes from clothing, footwear and other textile materials such as; household textiles, bags, rags, accessories, etc., which are generated by the citizenry in households, which are mostly pieces of clothing that are not worn because they have been damaged, have become small, or have gone out of fashion
  • Commercial textile waste. This is the textile waste of clothing, uniforms or safety accessories such as; gloves, caps, footwear, etc. or bedding and table or similar arising from catering activities, hospitality, hospitals and/or other commercial and service activities. This waste is assimilable to household municipal waste and can be managed through the same municipal channels, or can be managed by private managers (appropriate route when it is a large producer that generates a large amount of textile waste). It also includes textile waste from unsold items (pre-consumption), if managed as waste directly by textile sales establishments.

Finally, textile products are normally reusable, however it is possible to find single use textile products for:

  • Healthcare/Hospital: Sheets, gowns, shoe covers, maks, hats and mats to ensure hygiene in medical settings.
  • Food industry: Blouses, shoe covers, caps and other items to maintain hygiene in food preparation and handling.
  • Personal hygiene: Baby diapers, female compresses, incontinence products and toilet cloths.
  • Catering and events: Individual tablecloths, table paths, tablecloth coils and other items for events and celebrations.
  • Transportation: Slings for the transport of loads, straps for packaging and other products for the industry.
  • ...

Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for Textile products

The "Extended producer responsibility for producers" means that the responsibility of the producers of the textile products (such as, fashion brands) does not end when the textile products are sold, but it will extend untill the management of the waste that these products will eventually generate at the end of their useful life.

Manufacturers or importers of textile products have two options to comply with the obligations of Extended Producer Responsibility:

  • By means of individual systems, which would ensure the return of their brand's products at the end of their useful life.
  • By means of collective systems (SCRAP, Sistemas Colectivos de Responsabilidad Ampliada del Productor), which are third-party companies responsible for fulfilling the collection and recycling obligations, upon payment of a contribution.

The specific requirements will vary from country to country, but typically, the key elements are:

  • Reporting. The reporting could be based on quantities (units) or weight (kg) of clothes placed on the market, material type, end-of-live management data, waste treatment processes.
  • Participation in take-back schemes for waste collection.
  • PRO Fees. Fee payment to the relevant PRO per unit or weight of textiles placed on the market.

Textile EPR in Spain. Draft Royal Decree

In Spain, according to the regulation 7/2022, "no later than three years after the entry into force of this law, extended producer responsibility regimes will be developed for textiles, furniture and fixtures, and non-packaging agricultural plastics in application of Title IV of this law". According to the the National plan for the prevention of wastes, the regulation definition for textile products is currently in process

Nevertheless, already today some SCRAPS have been already set up for the management of textile and footwear waste in order to be prepared for the upcoming regulation:

  • RE-VISTE. Collective System for fashion, footwear, sport, and household textile products. Subscribing companies include Decathlon, El Corte Inglés, H&M, IKEA, Inditex, KIABI, Mango, Sprinter, etc.
  • GERESCAL, Collective system for footwear products in the Spanish regions of Alicante, Balearic Islands, and Toledo.  Subscribing companies include Pikolinos, Pabloski, Mascaró, Callaghan, Unisa, Wonders, Mustang, Zahonero, Pons Quintana, etc.
  • ...

 

The MITECO recently opened for public consultation until September 2025 the draft law for the EPR Schema for Textiles and Footwear available in  "Consulta e información pública del proyecto de real decreto por el que se regulan los productos text...").

In the draft law, the following is defined:

  • Textile clothing products and clothing accessories for domestic use or other uses, where those products are of a similar nature and composition to those for household use, which fall within the scope of the extended liability regime. CN codes 61* (Apparel and clothing accessories, knitted or crocheted), 62* (Apparel and clothing accessories, other than knitted or crocheted), 6301 (Blankets and travel blankets, excl. 6301 10 00),  6302 (Bedding, table linen, toilet linen or kitchen linen)...
  • Footwear and clothing and clothing accessories for domestic use or other uses, where those products are of a similar nature and composition to those for household use, the main composition of which is not textile, which fall within the scope of the extended liability regime. CN codes 4203  (Apparel and clothing accessories, of leather or composition leather, excluding footwear and headgear and parts thereof, and articles of Chapter 95, for example, spinnerets, fencing masks),  6401 (Waterproof footwear with outer soles and uppers of rubber or plastics, the upper part of which has not been attached to the sole by sewing or by means of rivets, nails, screws, pins or similar devices, nor formed with different parts joined in the same manner),...
  • Producer of product. Any manufacturer, importer or distributor or other natural or legal person who, irrespective of the selling technique, fulfils one of the following conditions:
    • Is established in Spain and manufactures textile or footwear products under his own name or trademark, or orders his design or manufacture and supplies them for the first time under his own name or trademark in Spain; or
    • Is established in Spain and resale within the national territory, under its own name or trade mark, textile or footwear products manufactured by other producers referred to in paragraph 1, the name, mark or trade mark of which is not given on such textile and footwear products; or
    • Is established in Spain and supplies, on a professional basis, for the first time in the national territory a textile or footwear product from another Member State or from a third country; or
    • Is established in another Member State or in a third country and sells textile or footwear products by means of distance communication directly to the end user, whether a particular household or not.
  • Registration. A new section in the MITECO registry will be created for Textiles and Footwear
  • Annual information. (by 31st march): Producers shall report annually the quantities by weight and number of articles of textile and footwear products placed on the market, indicating their CN code in accordance with the registration options in the Product Producers Register.
  • Invoicing obligations. 
    • The Producer’s MITECO Registration Number for textile and footwear shall be printed in the sales invoices
    • Producers of textiles and footwear shall include the contribution to the different collective EPRS in the invoices

 

References

  • Draft Royal Decree for Textiles and footwear EPR
  • Circular economy for textiles: taking responsibility to reduce, reuse and recycle textile waste and boosting markets for used textiles
  • Overview of all Textile Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) Laws
  •  EU strategy for sustainable and circular textiles
  • MITECO textile and footwear fractions
  • ECODE Law 7/2022 on wasted and circular economy from the textile point of view
  • Textile product codes:
    • https://www.boe.es/buscar/doc.php?id=DOUE-L-2018-80217
    • https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2017/354/oj/eng
  • The full list of textile fibres is defined in Real Decreto 928/1987

 

Disclaimer

  • This blog post is published for information purposes only and does not establish any commitment by SAP to deliver the contents defined in this publication.
  • The links external to SAP may change or cease working without any notice.

entry.title: Not getting calendar email notification
entry.content:
One of our team member is not getting SAC calendar notifications.

We have checked the profile settings in SAC settings and the system notifications and calendar notifications checkbox is enabled there.

Checked the Junk/Spam folder in Outlook. No trace of any mail.

What could be the issue

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Read XML File Maximum call stack size exceeded
entry.content: "Hello, I'm experiencing the same problem. Could you please share how you resolved it?"

entry.title: Re: SAP Sybase ASE Audit Logs: Handling Multi-Row Single...
entry.content:
To handle split extrainfo entries in Sybase ASE audit logs:
1. Use the audit_process_event function or ASE audit tools like sp_audit_read to reconstruct full events—these tools auto-merge split rows.
2. If writing custom SQL, identify related rows using event, eventtime, and spid, then use FOR XML PATH or STRING_AGG (if supported) to combine them.
3. Example (pseudocode):


SELECT eventtime, loginname,
STRING_AGG(extrainfo, '')
FROM sybsecurity..sysaudits_01
GROUP BY eventtime, loginname;


4. ASE does not support increasing the extrainfo column size—it’s fixed by Sybase.
5. Best practice: Use auditing tools or export logs and reassemble them via a script (Python/PowerShell) for complex cases.

entry.title: Re: Event when Workitem is generated for a User
entry.content:
Yes, this can be achieved using SAP Business Workflow Exit (SWE\_EVENT\_CREATE / SWE\_EVENT\_CREATE\_IN\_UPD\_TASK) or a Custom Event Receiver.
Implement a custom Event Linkage with a receiver function module that triggers on work item creation.
Within the FM, retrieve the WI\_AGENT (user responsible) from the container.
Then call your external UI application via HTTP/REST Proxy using CL_HTTP_CLIENT.
Register this linkage for all required events at runtime using SWE2.

entry.title: Re: 1 Project for multiple profit centers?
entry.content: Thanks, Chuma! I understand that Internal Orders are not in SAP Public Cloud anymore.

entry.title: Re: I cannot make comments on my questions.

entry.title: Re: Splash Screen - SAP BTP Mobile Service - Not Working...
entry.content: Hi Bill, thanks for your response. We are working in the Europe (Frankfurt) region, and currently we don’t see the option for version 25.6.0. Could it be that it hasn't been enabled for this region yet, or is there something we need to do on our side to make it available? I’ve updated the post and added an image at the end showing the versions currently available in Mobile Services. If you could help us by explaining how you enabled this version or where we should do it, it would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Embedded launch activities for SAP S/4HANA PCE for Extended Warehouse Management extra stack
entry.content:
Have you recently subscribed to SAP EWM extra stack and are gearing up for a project implementation? Starting off on the right foot is crucial to ensuring a smooth and successful SAP experience. That’s why SAP launched this Customer Onboarding Journey, specifically for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Extended Warehouse Management, extra stack. This journey provides the foundational steps, resources, and support you need to drive your implementation with clarity and confidence. 

What’s Your Next Step? 

1. Embedded launch activities 

If you’ve signed your SAP EWM Extra-Stack contract after April 15th 2025, you’re entitled to the embedded launch activities. A no-cost onboarding framework based on 3 pillars: 

  • Onboarding Journey 
  • Guided Enablement
  • Adoption Touchpoint 

Your journey begins with an onboarding experience guided by an assigned Onboarding Advisor, ensuring you're set up for success from day one. 

      2. Explore the SAP EWM Onboarding Resource Center 

Your centralized hub for a smooth onboarding experience. Access open enablement resources, including:  

  • Step-by-step guidance on using SAP for Me 
  • System access instructions 
  • Clear expectations and preparation tips for your implementation project 

       3. Join the Onboarding Webcasts 

These quarterly live sessions are designed to guide you through the Onboarding Resource Center, provide key insights, answer your questions, and help you quickly get up to speed with SAP EWM.  

  • Can’t make it live? Don’t worry—on-demand recordings are available whenever you need them. 

  

Your Onboarding Journey: Designed for Success 

The SAP EWM onboarding journey is carefully structured to provide value from contract start to Go-Live and beyond. It consists of three core elements: 

  • Onboarding Sessions 
  • Self-Paced Learning (Guided Enablement) 
  • Continuous Quality Checks (CQC) 

 

Let’s break each phase down. 

 

  1. Onboarding Sessions (Live 1:1 Virtual Meetings) 

 Shortly after your contract begins, an SAP Onboarding Advisor will invite your team (and your partner, if applicable) to two live virtual sessions: 

  • Session 1: Welcome to SAP 
    • Introduction to your onboarding journey 
    • System provisioning and user setup 
    • Explanation of your solution entitlements 
  • Session 2: Implementation Preparation 
    • Best practices for project execution 
    • SAP Cloud ALM, Enterprise Support Overview and Business AI  

📌Target Audience: IT contact, business process owners, project leads, implementation partners 

  

  1. Guided Enablement (Self-Paced Learning Courses) 

 To empower your team further, SAP offers a 4-hour Cloud Onboarding for SAP EWM course via SAP Learning. This course covers:  

  • Get Started: S-User creation, system access, SAP for Me, SAP Trust Center 
  • Plan Your Implementation: Activate methodology, Cloud ALM, testing and integration 
  • Get Enabled: SAP Help Portal, Business AI, value maps 
  • Prepare to Go-Live: CQC planning, ALM monitoring, upgrade strategy 

 📌 Ideal for: Project teams including IT, admins, partners, and business users 

📎Access here: SAP Learning – Cloud Onboarding for EWM  

  

  1. Adoption Touchpoint (Continuous Quality Check for Private Cloud Go-Live) 

 The CQC for Private Cloud Go-Live is a two-session remote service evaluating your system’s readiness: 

  • Pre-Go-Live Analysis: 3–4 weeks before go-live 
  • Post-Go-Live Verification: 6 weeks after go-live 

 Each session results in a prioritized action plan to address system stability, performance, and optimization according to SAP Best Practices.  

📎More info: CQC Info Sheet (SAP) 

  

Ready to Begin? 

Don’t wait until challenges arise - start your onboarding journey today to build a strong foundation for your SAP EWM implementation. 

 

📩Have questions or need support? Contact your Account Executive, Onboarding Advisor or visit the SAP EWM Onboarding Resource Center.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition, VAT return based on...
entry.content:
Hello @rekafulop ,

Configuring VAT Return on a Cash Basis in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Germany localization) involves a series of steps across financial accounting (FI), tax configuration, and possibly customer-specific extensions depending on how strictly you need to follow cash-based accounting for VAT.

Here’s a high-level process/steps you need to follow on configuration and processing VAT on a cash basis in S/4HANA Cloud for Germany:

First of all you need to have basic understanding of VAT Cash Basis in Germany. In Germany, VAT on a cash basis (Ist-Versteuerung) means VAT is only due when the payment is received or made, not when the invoice is issued or received. This is supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, but it requires:

  1. Special tax codes configured for cash-based VAT.
  2. Activation of cash-based VAT in the customer/vendor master.
  3. Adjustments in tax reporting logic (e.g., for the UStVA – German VAT return).

Now, lets go through Configuration Steps in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:-


A. Define Cash-Based Tax Codes
Use App: Manage Your Solution → Configure Your Solution

SSCUI / Configuration Step:

Country/Region: Germany (DE)

Activity: "Define Tax Codes for Sales and Purchases"

Set up special cash-based tax codes. Mark the tax code for cash-based accounting using the "Cash-based Tax" indicator.

B. Assign Cash-Based VAT Indicator in Customer/Vendor Master
Use App: Manage Business Partner Master Data

Go to Company Code Data tab.

Activate the flag: "Cash-Based Tax Accounting".

This setting is required for customers/suppliers to post VAT only when the payment is made/received.

C. Activate Cash-Based VAT in Posting Schemas
This is mostly handled via pre-delivered configuration in S/4HANA Cloud. If needed, it is controlled via:

  • Posting keys & rules
  • Output tax/ input tax determination
  • Accruals and deferrals for VAT

D. Ensure Payment Transactions Use the Cash Tax Codes
When a payment is made via Post Outgoing Payments or Manage Automatic Payments, ensure the cash-based VAT logic is triggered.

Now, Processing  and posting a transaction VAT on a Cash Basis

  • When the invoice is posted → no VAT entry is recorded yet.
  • When payment is posted → VAT is triggered, and then it's recorded in tax reports (e.g., UStVA).
  • Use the "Manage Journal Entries" and "Display Line Items" to confirm tax is only posted at time of payment.

How to file German VAT Return (UStVA)
Use App: VAT Return – Germany

Make sure only paid transactions appear in the report if using cash-based VAT.

The system automatically considers the cash indicator when aggregating values.

Use the tax codes and cash-based setup to extract correct tax base and tax amounts.

Reporting & Reconciliation
Use the following apps:

  • Manage Tax Items
  • Display Line Items in General Ledger
  • Run Advanced Compliance Reports (ACR) – German VAT return (UStVA)

Make sure the cash-based transactions reconcile with payments and tax reporting.

Optional: Customization & BAdIs (Advanced Use)
If needed, you can enhance behavior using:

BAdI: BADI_FQST_CASH_BASIS_TAX

To customize cash-based tax handling during postings.

Note: In S/4HANA Cloud, many BAdIs and exits are limited compared to On-Premise.

 

I hope this will answer your question.

Don't hesitate to reply if you need further support.

If you find my response useful, I kindly request you to award kudos as a small gesture. This motivates me to serve you better.

Appreciate your feedback.

Thanks,

Divyesh

entry.title: Change CPQ data center from German to Singapore
entry.content: Seeks expert guidance how to secure the existing CPQ customer business continuity while they asked to change their CPQ data center from German to Singapore for better performance. Any reference case is warmly appreciated.

entry.title: OData V2 Adapter is not working in Latest CDS Version 9
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I had created a sample CAP JAVA project by referring this link https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-cap-java-app-studio.html it worked fine in OData adapter V4. Then I changed the OData adapter version from v4 to v2 by referring this link https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/java/migration#v2adapter While I am trying access the metadata I am facing "Service Unavailable" Exception. Is OData V2 is not supported for latest CDS Versions?

entry.title: Re: Top menu toolbar is not clickable
entry.content: Just an update: the problem was solved by updating my SAP GUI to version 8.00.

entry.title: Timestamp not appearing in PROJ table
entry.content:
Hi All,

Timestamp is not appearing in the PROJ table. This is related to the table field CPD_UPDAT. It is blank when a project is created as well as released and is not filled up anytime.

Whereas the related WBS of the project all have values in the timestamp field and is available in PRPS. What might be the reason? There are some historical projects where it has the some values but I do not know what has got changed recently and for all projects this field is not appearing anymore in PROJ.

Regards,

Suman Misra

entry.title: Join Us Live: Ingesting SAP S/4HANA Master Data into SAP Customer Data Platform
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business environment, efficiently managing and integrating customer data is key to improving operations and enhancing customer experiences. Our upcoming live session, "Ingesting SAP S/4HANA Master Data into SAP Customer Data Platform," is designed to help you navigate this critical aspect of data management.

Session Overview

Scheduled for August 27th, this 1.5-hour session will provide a practical demonstration of integrating SAP S/4HANA Master Data with SAP CX Customer Data Platform. Participants will gain a clear understanding of the steps involved in the full integration setup and its execution. The session aims to equip you with the knowledge needed to utilize customer data effectively in both B2B and B2C scenarios.

Key Objectives

  1. Understanding Integration Endpoints: Explore the integration endpoints of SAP S/4HANA and SAP Customer Data Platform. This foundational knowledge is crucial for establishing efficient connectivity between these platforms.

  2. Ingesting Sample Master Data: Learn how to ingest sample SAP S/4HANA Master Data into SAP CDP. The session will cover the technical aspects of data ingestion, offering practical insights.

  3. Generating Insights and Activating Data: Discover how to use ingested master data to generate actionable insights. We'll show you how to activate S/4HANA Master Data for effective B2B and B2C scenarios using Customer Data Profiles, Accounts, and Activities.

Who Should Attend?

This session is geared towards IT professionals, consultants, data engineers, and system integrators. If you're looking to improve your data strategy and understand the value of SAP integration, this session will provide valuable insights.

Join Us

Take this opportunity on August 27th to enhance your understanding of SAP systems and refine your approach to customer data management. This session is an ideal fit for those looking to deepen their knowledge and practical skills in SAP integration.

Register Here: Ingesting SAP S/4HANA Master Data into SAP Customer Data Platform

This live session offers a straightforward look at integrating and activating SAP S/4HANA Master Data. Join us to gain the insights needed to optimize your data-driven strategies. We hope to see you there.

entry.title: Re: Splash Screen - SAP BTP Mobile Service - Not Working...
entry.content: We are still working on deploying MDK 25.6.x to Cloud Build so you would need to create your client locally at this point in time.

entry.title: Re: I cannot make comments on my questions.
entry.content: Hmmm than maybe I am confused with the new look but in the past each comment could have seperate response. So I could reply directly to your comment.

entry.title: Create Maintenance Plan App - missing fields in intial screen and Maint plan categ SO is missing
entry.content: In Create Maintenance Plan App, selection fields are missing in the initial screen and maintenance plan catergory SO is missing when tcode IP41 is used.

entry.title: Re: Event when Workitem is generated for a User
entry.content: As written, Custom Event Linkage is not working, since the Interface IF_WORKFLOW is missing in the Event class.

entry.title: Re: Timestamp not appearing in PROJ table
entry.content:
Hi,

Please refer to this note if on S/4HANA

The CPD_UPDAT field will not be updated in the PROJ table if the business function /CPD/CA_CPD_PFP_1 is not activated. This field is filled with the current timestamp only when the business function is active. 

3475431 - CPD_UPDAT field is updated without active PFP switch

See older notes if you have older versions

2224759 - PROJ Table Not Updated with Latest Timestamp 

Check in this method:

/CPD/CL_IM_PFP_NWA_UPDT  IF_EX_WORKORDER_UPDATE~AT_SAVE

If you use a custom logic to create the project or enhancements,  check with the ABAP team to ensure the field is populated.

entry.title: Hello SAP Community!
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

Let me introduce myself! I'm a SAP Developer working in Utrecht, the Netherlands. Last year I've worked with SAPUI5 (Freestyle and Fiori Elements), ABAP CDS, OData V2 and V4, Adobe Forms, CAP, ABAP and RAP. Currently I'm working on a blog about Adobe Forms: Classic v.s. Modern methods, so I'll keep you posted on that!

Best, Max

entry.content:
Hello,

In dropship process, we have PO and Statistical GR and Supplier invoice. If the PO price and Invoice price is different, it should hit the PPV. 

Can this be configured via standard SAP or need to do customization?

entry.title: Uncheck Long-term planning: single-item planning
entry.content:
Hi SAP Friends !

Any ideas or answers if it should be possible to uncheck

"Long-term planning: single-item planning in scenario" in an existing Long Term Planning scenario?

The checkbox is not active. If I create a new scenario, the checkbox can be checked/unchecked.

Thanks for any input.

Michael

entry.title: BR J_1BBR20: Mapping Field `ITEMS_E-FILLER19` to `FEBEP-KIDNO` in Electronic Bank Statement
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm evaluating modify FEBEP Mapping.  The FEB* tables are populated when importing the electronic statement, in This is BR localization, layout CNAB_240, imported through report RFEBKA00 > PERFORM format_itau(j_1bbr20) > J_1BBR20.

I am particularly interested in the information located in positions 202 to 240 of the bank statement file. During debugging, I discovered that this data is initially stored in the BDATA_AP string and later used to populate the ITEMS_E internal table. The specific value I need is in the 'ITEMS_E-FILLER19' field. However, I could not find a standard table where this field is stored. I was considering use FEBEP-KIDNO for this, because the mapping of the FEBEP table is done in perform insert_febep_e, I have 3 fields with the same information:
febep-chect = items_e-xblnr
febep-kidno = items_e-xblnr
febep-xblnr = items_e-xblnr

Is this replacement possible? Has anyone implemented a BAdI or user exit to achieve this?

Thanks in advance for any help or guidance!

Best Regards,

Att

RL

entry.title: A Beginners guide to SAP BDC
entry.content:
Many developers have contacted me in message box asking how to start with learning SAP BDC.

SAP Has provided many learning paths for same which are available on the Learning hub, but, what about people who don’t have access to learning hub or someone who is still working in legacy technologies.

Sharing these learning links hoping that this will help atleast a few people who trying to learn SAP BDC.

 

Core Components of SAP BDC system

Layer

Description

Source Systems

Includes SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, and external systems. Data is bundled into entities like tables and views for replication.

Foundation Services

Harmonizes and transforms raw data into Data Products. These are stored in a hyperscaler environment (SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake files).

SAP Datasphere

Acts as the semantic layer. Defines data models, manages access control, and supports both prebuilt and custom analytical scenarios.

SAP Databricks

Embedded AI/ML engine for advanced analytics. Enables pro-code engineering and machine learning extensions.

SAP Analytics Cloud

Provides visualization, planning, and ad-hoc analysis capabilities. Powers dashboards and intelligent applications.

BDC Cockpit

Central hub for managing Data Products, Intelligent Applications, and data sharing workflows.

 

You must learn SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics cloud to be ready for SAP BDC.

 

You may use below link to learn more about SAP Datasphere.

Get Started with Your Trial in SAP Datasphere | SAP Tutorials

This mission is the ideal starting point for newcomers to start using SAP Datasphere, get their trial account, and learn the fundamentals for modelling their data in SAP Datasphere.

This training will cover below activities.

  1. Get to Know the SAP Datasphere Interface | SAP Tutorials : talks about how to navigate the user interface of SAP Datasphere and what the home screen controls can do for you
  2. Add Users and Assign Roles in SAP Datasphere | SAP Tutorials : Talks about how to add users into your SAP Datasphere tenant, how to assign roles to users, How to edit users in bulk
  3. Create and Monitor a Space in SAP Datasphere | SAP Tutorials : Talks about how to create a Space and how to manage and monitor your Space
  4. Create an Entity Relationship Model in SAP Datasphere | SAP Tutorials : Talks about overview of E/R Models, how to create an E/R Model, how to associate data columns and how to finalise an E/R Model and deploy
  5. Create a Graphical View in SAP Datasphere | SAP Tutorials : talks about graphical views, how to create them, how to rename and restore columns in Graphical view and their deployment
  6. Define Measures and Add Business Information to a View | SAP Tutorials talks about what is semantic information, how to define measures, how to add business information, how to change business names of measures and attributes, how to change the semantic type of measures and attributes and how to preview your modelled dataset
  7. Generate Time Table and Dimensions | SAP Tutorials talks about how to create time tables and dimensions and how to associate the time dimensions with your data
  8. Upload Flat Files and Wrangle Your Data | SAP Tutorials : Talks about how to upload flat files in the Data Builder of SAP Datasphere, how to make the best of the Data Builder capabilities during a file upload process and how to transform the data for data consistency and integrity
  9. Replicate Data into SAP Datasphere | SAP Tutorials : Talks about how to check the type of data access, how to create a real time data replication and how to schedule the data replication
  10. Integrate and Monitor Data with SAP Datasphere | SAP Tutorials : Talks about how to access the Data Integration Monitor, how to use the Data Integration Monitor dashboard and the types of monitoring you can do with the Data Integration Monitor

 

 

You can use below links to know more about SAP Analytics cloud.

  1. Exploring SAP Analytics Cloud
  2. Create a Live Data Connection in SAP Analytics Cloud | SAP Tutorials
  3. Create Live Data Model and a Story | SAP Tutorials
  4. Develop and Consume Queries on SAP Analytics Cloud | SAP Tutorials

SAP has basic trial versions for both SAP Datasphere and SAC where you may get some hands on knowledge to perform few activities.

Get started with Basic Trial | SAP Tutorials

 

Once you have a good understanding of SAP Datasphere and SAC, you can start going through SAP BDC learning paths.

Introducing SAP Business Data Cloud

 

You may also go through below blogs

SAP BDC for Dummies - SAP Community

SAP BDC Key Features - SAP Community

Deep Dive into SAP BDC Data products - SAP Community

 

Happy Learning!

 

Swati Gawade-Shingade.

Cognizant technology Solutions.

entry.title: Re: How to limit a specific group's access to learning (L...
entry.content:
Hi Sarah, 

We've done exactly this before, and we did exactly what you described with the permission roles, as well as updating the connector settings.

For the permission roles, prior to this new requirement, we had a catch-all role that all users had which granted the Learning Access Permission. So we removed it from this catch-all role and created two new permission roles (and two new permission groups), one with the Learning Access Permission and one without it. Our requirement was based on 'Payroll Area' which was easy enough to manage via the RBP group rulings. 

We then updated the connector feed from Integration Center to exclude a particular Payroll Area. That way, they never touched the LMS at all. We did all this before creating the users in Employee Central so didn't need to unpick anything for users already in the LMS thankfully. It was actually all quite straightforward!

entry.title: How to migrate a Central Repository?
entry.content:
How to migrate a Central Repository from old servers to new servers?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to migrate a Central Repository?
entry.content:
According to note 2768127:

Work before migration:

  1. Make sure all <SOURCE DATABASE> local repositories have same/latest version of code/objects prior to migration.
  2. Please create blank databases/schemas on <TARGET DATABASE> server where local and central repositories will be moved/migrated. Appropriate permissions set, database user should be schema/DB owner.

Step to migrate central repositories:

A central repository cannot be migrated like a local repository can be migrated. That's why it is ensured in step 1 (Work before migration), so that <SOURCE DATABASE> central repository can be discontinued.

In case source and target databases of central repositories are the same (both have to be <SOURCE DATABASE> or both <TARGET DATABASE>), central repository can be preserved by copying database directly. In this case, it is moving from <SOURCE DATABASE> to <TARGET DATABASE>, the only way to move using all existing objects is using some external tools to move data from <SOURCE DATABASE> to <TARGET DATABASE>. Otherwise, starting with a fresh/blank central repository should be considered.

  1. From DS Repository Manager create a new secured central <TARGET DATABASE> repository (in the blank schema created in Step 2 (Work before migration))
  2. Configure the <TARGET DATABASE> central repository in the CMC (Created in Step 2 above)
  3. From Data Services Management Console activate the central repository
  4. From DS designer connect to central repository and start using normally. The Central repository will be empty, and objects can be checked in and subsequently checked ou from it.

NOTE: Do not copy and restore local or central repositories between different server machines.

entry.title: Re: SAP BI 2025: What’s New In Web Intelligence and Semantic Layer
entry.content: Great post. Thank you very much for all these updates.

entry.title: Re: SAP Story Reports Input Parameters on Calculated Columns Expression

entry.title: ABAP RAP to do CRUD operation on SAP Standard tables
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Straight to the point: I want to use ABAP RAP to create an OData based API for SAP Cost Allocations (T811C, T811S, T811K, ....). I have a bunch of questions that your comments will be highly appreciated upon them.

Q1: Is the chosen way the correct way to do CRUD operation on SAP Standard tables? I have chosen the way because SAP do not offer BAPIs for Cost Allocations (Cycles).

Q2: How to differentiate between CO-PA allocations and CCA allocations?

Q3: I want to handle the authorization based on "RK" + IPKNZ + ALART fields which comes out of T811C table. In this way I will have the exact Business Transaction for CO_VRGNG field of K_VRGNG authorization object. Although this method may work for CCA allocation, is there any method to check authorization for CO-PA allocations?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Po tax should populate po header text
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please check the documentation and possibilities with Custom Logic, that is easily accesible with Public Cloud:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/57909455bf7c4fdd8bcf48d76c1eae33.html?locale=en-US&q=custom+logic

Also look at BAdI extensibility:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/ace42ede68ef4457937749326f936ac1.html

Hope it will help you! 

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Configure Relative Dates in Manage Route Maps for a 360 Review
entry.content:
I am trying to configure the relative dates for each stage in the 360 review process to the following. How do I do that? Note, the dates listed are just examples. I want the configuration to be relative to the start date of the assessment. This configuration is editable in Manage Route Maps section of SuccessFactors.

Step Name                                                    Step Start Date              Due Date               Step Days
1 Employee Selects Participant               04/15/2025                  04/22/2025           7 days
2 Manager Approves Participant             04/22/2025                  04/29/2025           7 days
3 ELP Approves Participant                       04/29/2025                  05/06/2025              7 days
4 Raters Provide Feedback                       05/06/2025                    05/20/2025          14 days
5 Completed — —

entry.title: Re: HCM Brasil - Atualização das tabelas de IRRF
entry.content:
Boa Tarde Gulherme

Há alguma Nota SAP para este processo no Módulo de FI?

Att

Leandro

entry.title: Re: Return of Inventory in Transit
entry.content:
Hello, 

The best way would be to receipt them at the warehouse and than create return delivery or reverse STO. If you don't have EWM active, only MM-IM, you should be able to reverse the movement just for this 2 items and check the STO as finished. The solution depends on what happend to this materials. If they are distroyed you should be able to receipt them in warehouse but not on stock but for scrap, if they are lost you should also have respective account to post it to that account. 

Please let me know if you need any more quidance on this matter.

Regards, 

Jagoda

entry.title: Calculation technique in SAP SD
entry.content: Hi Gurus, I am looking at a way to replicate this invoice with these calculations in SAP SD as per requirement from client. Please share some advice on how to achieve this.

entry.title: Re: Professional Service : Copy Project with Staffed Reso...
entry.content:
Hi, 

If the external staffing is active , then the resources do not get copied when the project is copied because the data is managed externally and subject to availability, approval and planning rules in the external RM.

Best Regards,

Shilpa

entry.title: Re: Change attendance list sorting order in SAP SuccessFa...
entry.content: @imit, as it stands, that wouldn't be possible. There's an open enhancement request similar to your scenario that could be reviewed and voted on.

entry.title: Re: An Easy Way to Use SAP CAP to Call a REST API
entry.content:
@Nickfury 

CAP for Node.js natively supports REST API calls and enables the use of destinations configured in the BTP cockpit.

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/using-services#mock-remote-service-as-odata-service-node-js

Example:

https://qiita.com/tami/items/01160bb22c5453bb69cb#2-openai%E3%81%AErest-api%E3%82%92%E4%BD%BF%E3%81%86

entry.title: Re: Return of Inventory in Transit

entry.title: Manage Purchase Order app does not show custom document types
entry.content:
Hi,

We are working s4core 2021 private cloud edition that was converted from ECC. and we are facing an issue where the "Manage Purchase Order" Fiori app does not show custom document types. I have tried changing the field selection of the custom document types to the same field selection in standard document type NB as well. However, I still cannot see the custom document types in the app.

Please advise.

Thanks,

Ruwani

entry.title: Re: User SAPSR3DB does not exist during an SAP Netweaver...
entry.content: I managed to surpass this by uninstalling everything, and then, i just installed Oracle software (without database).

entry.title: Re: User SAPSR3DB does not exist during an SAP Netweaver...
entry.content:
I managed to surpass this by uninstalling every trace of Oracle database, and then, just install Oracle software (without database). This is done right in the 2nd step of Oracle installation:

After this, SWPM installation just installed Oracle as it should, so, thank you @mamartins for showing me the light!

entry.title: Re: Cannot update Picklist after updating source document
entry.content:
Hi,

You PATCH the order to increase the quantity is correct. But the released quantity is not updated automatically. You need to patch the released quantity before update to the picklist. Refer below

PATCH /b1s/v2/PickLists(679)
{
  "PickListsLines": [
    {
      "LineNumber": 0,
      "ReleasedQuantity": 5.2
    }
  ]
}

entry.title: Re: Prorate based on Months not days
entry.content:
You can customize in the subscription only the price condition as such, but not the pricing logic in the pricing tree.

With that, the logic to calculate the proration factor is not affected. So there should not be a difference if the price condition was fetched from a lookup table or if it was customized in the subscription. 

Did you check the pricing logic in the analyze pricing document app?
There you can also define on price element level values for the price condition if defined in the price element specification.

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Marssel Vilaça
entry.content: Excellent! A great friend and a great colleague in the SAP Community.

entry.title: "strange" dumps HTTP_SEND_ERROR happening once per minute
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We're having "strange" dumps HTTP_SEND_ERROR happening once per minute:

We also see other occurrences of this in SCOT (Administration>Inbound Send Requests). Once per minute, a request is generated, always from the same sender to the same recipient, and e-mail content (a Dunning Notice) is always the same. User doesn't have a clue how this started to happen suddenly and seem that lock him prevents this from happening, but not a proper solution.

We checked SCOT, SOST, SBWP, several logs, program debug, and contacted different functional people, but no success.

Hope someone has any leads on how to identify the root cause.

entry.title: Re: Event when Workitem is generated for a User
entry.content:
Hi,

You can use the approach by @Sandra_Rossi . Make an implicit enhancement at the end of CREATE_VIA_WFM/CREATE_VIA_API  method. Define your custom workflow class ZCL_WF_EXT_UI_INT to implement the interface IF_WORKFLOW. Create event WORKITEM_CREATED in the custom class and methods to handle the HTTP request to external UI application. Create function module ZF_TRIGGER_EXT_UI with parameters actor_tab of type swhactor for the user(s) and all additional parameters required.  Maintain the linkage of custom workflow class event in SWE2 to your new workflow task. Trigger the WORKITEM_CREATED event of your custom class from your custom function module like below.

CALL METHOD cl_swf_evt_event=>get_event_container
  EXPORTING
    im_objcateg  = cl_swf_evt_event=>mc_objcateg_cl "Class wf 
    im_objtype   = lv_objtype "ZCL_WF_EXT_UI_INT
    im_event     = lv_event "WORKITEM_CREATED
  RECEIVING
    re_reference = lr_event_parameters. "required parameters for wf task


lv_param_name = "Parameter name.
"set all required parameters: 
TRY.
    CALL METHOD lr_event_parameters->set
      EXPORTING
        name       = lv_param_name
        value      = " parameter value.
CATCH cx_swf_cnt_cont_access_denied .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_access_denied .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_not_found .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_type_conflict .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_unit_type_conflict .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_def_invalid .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_container .

ENDTRY.

lv_param_name = 'ACTOR_TAB'.
TRY.
    CALL METHOD lr_event_parameters->set
      EXPORTING
        name       = lv_param_name
        value      = actor_tab.
CATCH cx_swf_cnt_cont_access_denied .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_access_denied .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_not_found .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_type_conflict .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_unit_type_conflict .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_elem_def_invalid .
  CATCH cx_swf_cnt_container .
"Handle events using custom exception class ZCX_EXT_UI_INT
ENDTRY.

TRY.
    CALL METHOD cl_swf_evt_event=>raise
      EXPORTING
        im_objcateg        = cl_swf_evt_event=>mc_objcateg_cl
        im_objtype         = lv_objtype "ZCL_WF_EXT_UI_INT
        im_event           = lv_event "WORKITEM_CREATED
        im_objkey          = lv_objkey
        IM_EVENT_CONTAINER = lr_event_parameters
        .
  CATCH cx_swf_evt_invalid_objtype .
  CATCH cx_swf_evt_invalid_event .
"Handle events using custom exception class ZCX_EXT_UI_INT
ENDTRY.


COMMIT WORK.

entry.title: Re: Blog It Forward - Marssel Vilaça
entry.content: Thank you my friend @MRobalinho

entry.title: Contract Termination-Derecognition of contract not posting on asset side
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I’m facing an issue when terminating a lease contract. The system does not post the derecognition of the contract on the asset side against the lease liability account. As a result, a balance remains in the asset clearing account. Additionally, the asset continues to show an incorrect planned depreciation amount.

I’ve encountered this behavior with two different clients. Is this considered standard system behavior, or is there something missing in the configuration or process? How can this issue be properly resolved?

Any insights or experiences would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: load child entity when Create button is clicked in RA...
entry.content: @Mikhail_Minakov Thank you for the solution. This worked!

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Accelerator-based UI storefront vs Composable storefront (Spartacus)
entry.content:
Does SAP support Commerce Cloud Accelerator UI storefront after 2027 ?

Should we shift to Composable storefront (Spartacus).

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Project Plan Cost are different
entry.content:
hello @Keratiya_D 

The difference between your 8M Financial Plan and 4M shown in the Project Costs app is usually due to below highlights

  • Different Plan Versions selected in each app
  • Filters applied (WBS elements, Cost Category, Fiscal Year)
  • Currency settings mismatch (Company Code vs. Project Currency)
  • Not all planned costs are assigned to visible WBS levels
  • Mixing of Plan, Actual, and Commitment values

What to do!

  • Check Plan Version consistency in both apps
  • Align filters and selection parameters
  • Ensure currency settings are the same
  • Review sub-WBS elements for hidden costs

Check below the SAP reference
3535188 - How to use APP 'Import Financial Plan Data'

Hope that helps!

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Re: Invoice Date Control While Creating Customer Invoice
entry.content:
Hello @serap_acar 

This classic BADI doesn't exist. The S4HANA Public Cloud has limited, predefined enhancement options, and currently, billing-related logic can't be changed using this specific BADI.

Your options for influencing billing processes are

  • Using the available Custom Logic enhancement points, but these are limited for SD Billing
  •  Leveraging APIs or Events via Communication Scenarios for external processing
  • Building side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP if your requirement is critical

If your project needs logic similar to SD_BILLING_DOCUMENT_CREATE, and it's not supported in Public Cloud as i previously ask you to check, you can Check the available enhancement options through the SAP Extensibility Catalogue

entry.title: Re: How to short automatic packing HU in putaway
entry.content:
Hello @imel_99 

The automatic HU packing depends on your active scope in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

If Warehouse Management (5DV) or Advanced Shipping & Receiving (2EJ) are activated, you can use Packaging Specifications to control how HUs are filled, typically prioritising full HUs first.

If only basic inbound processes are in scope, advanced HU split logic may not be available.

I recommend you check active scope items in CB and review the Packaging Specification setup for correct HU capacity and UoM settings

If full HU-first packing isn’t working despite proper setup, it may require a support ticket, as Public Cloud has limited custom logic for HU packing.

Check these SAP References:

SAP Help: Handling Units in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Best Practices for Warehouse Management - Public Cloud

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Unable to create follow-up service order via API_SERVICE_ORDER_SRV – linkage to parent missing
entry.content:
We are implementing service order creation in SAP S/4HANA 2023 (Private Cloud Edition) using the OData API `API_SERVICE_ORDER_SRV`. Our business requirement is to create a **follow-up service order** that links back to an existing (parent) service order, similar to document flow logic in SAP CRM.

We have tested the following fields during service order creation:

- `ReferenceServiceOrder` (header level)
- `ReferenceServiceOrderItem` (item level)
- `RefServiceOrderTemplate`
- `RefServiceOrderTemplateItem`

In all cases:
- The service order is created successfully, but no transactional linkage to the parent order is formed.
- No document flow appears in the UI or via API.
- The fields appear to behave as free-text reference fields with no functional impact.

We would like to confirm the following:

1. Is there a supported mechanism to create a **follow-up service order** (predecessor-successor relationship) using `API_SERVICE_ORDER_SRV`?
2. If not, is there an enhancement planned in a future release or a known workaround (e.g., BAdI, enhancement spot)?
3. Is there any supported way to reflect document flow between service orders — e.g., similar to CRM’s `CRMD_LINK` or ECC’s `VBFA`?

We are following clean-core principles and would like to avoid classic BAPI fallback unless absolutely necessary.

Example payload tested:
- Parent service order: `8000001234`
- Targeted linkage via fields like `ReferenceServiceOrder`, `RefServiceOrderTemplate`, etc.

Please advise on whether follow-up creation is supported via standard APIs or must be implemented via extensions.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation.ERROR_MSG_NO_METADATA when executing a form in SAP Build
entry.content:
I'm working on a student admission process built in SAP Build Process Automation, and I'm running into a metadata issue when the workflow gets triggered.

In the inbox, I get an error that says: ERROR_MSG_NO_METADATA, and the instance goes straight into ERRONEOUS status. I suspect the form was originally deployed in a public environment that's now deprecated.

Has anyone faced something similar? What’s the best way to recover the workflow or rebuild it so I can deploy in a shared environment without losing the design?

Thanks ! Any tips would be appreciated

entry.title: Re: Splash Screen - SAP BTP Mobile Service - Not Working...
entry.content: It is in testing now and if all goes well should be out by end of next week.

entry.title: [BYD] Mass Export of PDF documents of Scanned Invoices
entry.content:
Dear Support Team,

 

I have a request and query regarding the mass export of Supplier PDF invoices from the system.

However, while looking to export these, I could not figure out a possibility to do a mass export but only the possibility of individual export by going through each supplier invoices which is very tedious and time consuming.

Therefore, I wanted to ask if there is any way to fulfil this requirement of mass export?

 

Please do let me know about the possible solution.

 

Best Regards,

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: [BYD] Mass Export of PDF documents of Scanned Invoices
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

Thank you for reaching out. I have explored two potential methods to address your special requirements, but unfortunately, neither approach meets the criteria needed.

  •  **Data Extraction Method**: Creating a Data Extraction under the Data Extraction View of Application and User Management Work Center was attempted. However, there is no corresponding Business Object available for this specific task.
  • **Invoice Scanning Export**: Exporting the invoice scanning work list data to Microsoft Excel using the Export button in the Invoice Scanning view of the Supplier Invoicing work center. This method only exports the invoice scanning work list with the PDFs' names, which does not fulfill the requirement.

Given these limitations, the only viable solution appears to be manually processing each item individually. If you have any further questions or need additional assistance, please feel free to let me know. I am here to help. 

 

Best regards,

SAP Technical Support, Public Cloud ERP

entry.title: Re: How to short automatic packing HU in putaway
entry.content:
Hi Chuma,

Thank you for your advice. After I activate 5 DV & 2EJ, would you like to explain what the specific configuration is that allows me to maintain Packaging Specifications to control how HUs are filled, typically prioritising full HUs first? Because I can't find the specific way to maintain this here https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/8cd6fb022fa441fdb110fa511b2d4347.html?q=Handling+Units+

 

Regards, 

Melani

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (São Paulo, Brazil)
entry.content: Mais um ano Diogo! Partiuuuuuuuu!

entry.title: How to Manage Multi-User Conversation History in a RAG App with Generative AI Hub SDK
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have a question regarding application development using the Generative AI Hub.

Goal:
I am developing a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) application using the Generative AI Hub SDK and Flask (Python). My goal is to deploy this application on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry, and enable multiple users to access it concurrently.

Current Situation:

  • Architecture:
    • AI Model: Generative AI Hub
    • Orchestration: Using the Orchestration Workflow from the Generative AI Hub SDK
    • Application Framework: Flask (Python)
    • Deployment Target: SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry

The Challenge:
My main challenge is figuring out how to manage conversation history separately for each user when multiple users are interacting with the application simultaneously. The current implementation is nearly stateless, which means conversation history is either shared across all users or not maintained at all.

Questions:

  1. Managing Conversation History:
    What is the best practice for persisting user-specific conversation history (chat history) for a Flask application running on Cloud Foundry? Should I use an external storage service like SAP HANA Cloud or Redis to store the history, linking it to a session ID or user ID?
  2. Alternative Implementation Methods:
    Are there any simpler methods available within the Generative AI Hub SDK or LangChain to manage user-specific conversation context without relying on external storage?

Any advice, code examples, or documentation you could point me to would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your help.
